title: Mega Steelix Battle Guide
summary: A [steel]/[ground] physical fortress with 230 Defense and [sand-force] — the heaviest Pokemon in the game turns [heavy-slam] into a max-power STAB.
strengths: 230 Defense tanks almost any physical hit twice
[sand-force] gives 30% boost to [ground]/[steel]/Rock moves in sand
Doubles as a [stealth-rock] setter and damage dealer
weaknesses: Speed 30 forces trick-room or last-slot usage
230 Defense is useless against special attackers

recommendedMoves.reason: Max BP [steel] STAB thanks to 1480kg Mega weight
Reliable hazard setter given the slow speed
Secures Mega turn and scouts choice locks

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Steelix is a [steel]/[ground] physical wall defined by a 230 base Defense stat — one of the highest in the game. [sand-force] boosts [steel], [ground], and Rock moves by 30% under sand, letting Mega Steelix double as an offensive threat instead of a pure wall. Its 1480kg weight also guarantees the maximum damage tier on [heavy-slam].

## Strengths
230 Defense reduces almost every neutral physical hit to chip damage, and HP-invested spreads survive 2+ close-combats or [earthquake]s from top attackers. Under sand, [earthquake] reaches effective 130 BP and [heavy-slam] hits max weight BP, turning the wall into a credible attacker. The [steel]/[ground] typing resists [fairy], Flying, Rock, Electric (immune), and more — a wide defensive coverage.

## Weaknesses & Answers
4x weaknesses to Water, Fire, Fighting, and [ground] are all still live, and a hydro-pump or close-combat will still OHKO. Speed 30 makes it either a trick-room abuser or a slow pivot. Special attackers bypass the Defense stat entirely, so pair it with a special sponge.

## Recommended Moveset
[heavy-slam] is the locked [steel] STAB — the Mega form's 1480kg weight guarantees max BP against most targets. [earthquake] is the primary [ground] STAB and spreads damage in doubles. [stealth-rock] is the standard hazard option given the slow speed. [protect] secures the Mega turn and enables safe pivot plays.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[steelixite] is mandatory. [sand-force] activates after Mega Evolution. Tera [steel] adds resistances and patches the Fire weakness, while Tera [fairy] flips the Fighting 4x weakness into a resistance for a defensive reroute.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair with sand setters like tyranitar or hippowdon to activate [sand-force], and with trick-room leads like dusknoir or hatterene to bypass the Speed 30 problem. Cover the Water and Fighting weaknesses with Grass and Psychic partners respectively.

## Play Patterns
Do not lead — bring Mega Steelix in on a predicted switch or after trick-room/sand is up. Use [protect] on the Mega turn for safety, then start pressuring with [earthquake] and [heavy-slam]. Drop [stealth-rock] when the opponent is forced to pivot.
